name: Cajun Prime Rib
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the fat cap from the top of the meat (butcher can do this for you) and save it.
2. Place the roast, standing on the rib bones, in a very large roasting pan.
3. Make several punctures through the silver skin with a knife so seasoning can permeate the meat.
4. Pour a generous, even layer of black pepper over the top of the meat (the pepper should completely cover it).
5. Repeat steps 4 and 5 with garlic powder and salt, totally covering the preceding layer.
6. Carefully arrange the onions in an even layer on top so as not to knock off the seasoning.
7. Place the fat cap back on top. Refrigerate for 24 hours.
8. Bake ribs in a 550°F oven until the fat is dark brown and crispy on top, about 35 minutes.
9. Remove from oven and cool slightly.
10. Refrigerate until well chilled, about 3 hours (this is done so the juices will solidify and the steaks can be cooked rare).
11. Remove the fat cap and discard it.
12. Scrape off the onions and as much of the seasonings as possible with a large knife; discard.
13. Slice between ribs into 6 steaks (4 will have bones); trim the cooked surface of meat from the 2 pieces that were on the outside of the roast.
14. Season and cook in your favorite way for steaks.
15. To blacken the steaks, combine the ingredients of the seasoning mix thoroughly in a small bowl; you will have about 8 tablespoons.
16. Sprinkle the steaks generously and evenly on both sides with the mix (about 4 teaspoons per steak).
17. Heat a cast iron skillet over very high heat until it is beyond the smoking stage and you see white ash on the skillet bottom—this should take at least 10 minutes.
18. Place one steak in the hot skillet and cook only one side at a time; cook over very high heat until the underside starts to develop a heavy, black crust, about 2 to 3 minutes.
19. Turn the steak over and cook until the underside is crusted like the first, about 2 to 3 minutes more.
20. Repeat with the remaining steaks.
21. Serve each steak while piping hot.
(NOTE: If you don't have a commercial hood vent over your stove, this dish may smoke you out of the kitchen. It's worth it! But you can also cook it outdoors on a gas grill; a charcoal fire doesn't get hot enough to "blacken" the steak properly. If you have a smoke detector in your house, you will be able to determine if it is working correctly. This is NOT a dish to prepare in an apartment building with a central fire alarm system wired into your smoke detector. It causes great excitement! Also, you can be guaranteed you will meet your landlord.)
